dateline: Tokyo, Japan — BTS Closes Tokyo Dome 2-Night Run; ARIRANG Holds Billboard 200 No. 1 for Third Consecutive Week
heroHeadline: BTS Tokyo Dome Night 2 Live-Broadcast to 75+ Countries — ARIRANG Locks Historic 3rd Consecutive Billboard 200 No. 1
heroSubtitle: 4,692 days since debut: BTS completes the Tokyo Dome 2-night run on April 17–18, 2026 — first Japan concerts in ~7 years. ARIRANG holds No. 1 on the Billboard 200 for a third consecutive week (124K units, April 18 chart), the first K-pop album in history to do so. Night 2 is live-broadcast to 3,500+ cinemas in 75 countries. Tour continues through 34 cities in 23 countries through March 2027.
footerNote: Data sourced from HYBE, Billboard, Hanteo Chart, Gaon Chart, Melon, Spotify, Netflix, Guinness World Records, IFPI, Korea Times, Yonhap, setlist.fm, and Recording Academy.
